title: new 'no-dereference' mount option
body:
Add a new `no-dereference` mount option supported by crun 1.11+ to     
re-create/copy a symlink if it's the source of a mount.  By default the
kernel will resolve the symlink on the host and mount the target.      
As reported in #20098, there are use cases where the symlink structure 
must be preserved by all means.                                        

Fixes: #20098
Fixes: issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-1935

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

@giuseppe and I sat down again on this issue.  We decided to move the logic of copying/preserving the symlinks into `crun`.  Dealing with symlinks over symlinks potentially crossing volumes is very prone to errors when doing it "manually" in Podman.  The runtine can deal with it after having set up all the mounts with a much lower risk.

The PR here will still be needed but we need to wait for a new crun to land.
@cevich could you bump the VM images to include crun 1.11?  This PR needs functionality shipped with the new crun, and I'd love to get it in before Podman 4.8.

> bump the VM images to include crun 1.11

@containers/podman-maintainers Could somebody help out with this chore please?  My focus is currently elsewhere on another high-priority task.
I can work on it today, but if I don't finish today someone will need to finish it tomorrow (I'm OOTO all day Tuesday)
